text: Here is the endoscopic view of the lesion.
And here you can see the relation of the lesion with the structures of the posterior mediastinum.
We begin by opening the posterior mediastinum pleura below the azygous vein in order to circumferentially release the lesion.
We proceed with the dissection of the muscular layer of the esophagus
After obtaining the correct plane of dissection, we proceed with the lateral release of the laser.
At that moment, counter traction with the respirator through the system port helps us with this dissection.
until we progressively release the entire lesion from the esophagus.
After removing the surgical specimen, we proceeded with the closure of the muscular layer with interlipid observable suture.
The surgery was performed with a 32 French full-sheet gastric tube, which was pulled after surgery for the saline test, which was negative.
